library_preparation_preflight.summary: Meaningful coverage of spring-boot-flyway requires exercising Spring Boot Flyway auto-configuration against a real JDBC database; the resolved artifact brings spring-boot-jdbc and flyway-core but not spring-boot-autoconfigure or an embedded database driver.

deterministic_setup.reason: spring-boot-flyway's main value is its auto-configuration classes, which import and annotate against org.springframework.boot.autoconfigure types that are normally supplied by Spring Boot starters rather than by the spring-boot-flyway artifact itself.

Spring Boot's Flyway support runs migrations against a DataSource or spring.flyway URL; the artifact has Flyway and JDBC integration but no database driver, while Spring Boot 4.0.6 dependency management uses H2 2.4.240 for embedded database tests.

library_preparation_preflight.agent_guidance: Exercise the auto-configuration with an in-memory JDBC URL/DataSource and a real migration script under the default `classpath:db/migration` location, then assert that Flyway runs the migration and records/applies it. Keep the test focused on Spring Boot's Flyway integration rather than testing Flyway core alone.
risks: A generated test that only instantiates FlywayProperties or Flyway core APIs may compile but will not meaningfully cover the spring-boot-flyway integration.
Native Image and local Gradle verification remain authoritative for whether H2-based coverage needs additional metadata or adjustment.
